plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-404 RP13.P06.M01.S13-RP13.P06.M03.S12 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-404 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP13.P06 contract implementation slice: the M01 Contract Draft tail (the eight remaining rows), the full M02 Type And Shape Definition cycle (twenty rows), and the M03 Primary Implementation Slice head (the first twelve rows) as three descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H13), Claude review packet (C13), and CP00-405 handoff, without opening payments, payment matching, AR aging, or journal entry runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-404 is a clean, descriptor-only closeout pack for the RP13 payments-core P06 contract implementation slice (RP13.P06.M01.S13-RP13.P06.M03.S12, risk B, 40 units), following the established CP392-CP403 pattern. All eight in-scope files (contract JSON, README, index/registry/service/validators, test, validator script) are internally consistent. The CP404 pack binding, requirements, no-write attestation, descriptor/case-set builders, coverage/descriptor validators, and Hermes/Claude/handoff packet builders all align with the baseline: risk_class B, unit_count 40, range RP13.P06.M01.S13-RP13.P06.M03.S12, next_pack CP00-405, next_subphase RP13.P06.M03.S13. Deliverable distribution {ui:8, claude_review:2, security_audit:10, test:4, implementation:16} and micro-title distribution {Contract Draft:8, Type And Shape Definition:20, Primary Implementation Slice:12} match the baseline and sum to 40; required_section_rows enumerate exactly the 40 planned unit titles and validatePaymentsCoreCp404Coverage enforces every distribution/section check. The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly NOT asserted (production_ready:false, status implemented_pending_review); production_ready_flag is a stage label, not a trust assertion. No-write/no-runtime/no-real-data/no-leak guards are uniformly set across registry attestation, service freezeCp404Result, contract projection, and manifest boundary. Authority boundary is preserved: claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true; the Claude review packet is read_only with allowed tools Read/Grep/Glob. CP178+ review hardening is encoded via forbidden_review_evidence (auth-failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped, missing fields, claude final-approval claim, enterprise-trust claim) and hardened_review_sequence; readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON; pack_level_claude_review status pending with empty valid_review_receipts and invalid_review_attempts; post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) correctly do not yet exist. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packet/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime. command-evidence records all 25 validation commands at exit 0 (node --check/--test, rp05-rp13 validators, closeout-pack-plan/weighted/spec/rp00-control-plane/goal-closeout/fullplan validators, npm test, npm run validate, npm run build, git diff --check), with closeout-pack:validate correctly gated to post-review. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; the descriptor-only implementation is sound, bounded, and validated. This review is a read-only independent reviewer verdict and does not grant final approval or enterprise trust, which require human sign-off.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-404 payments core p06 contract implementation slice descriptor pack
